headlines.title: 'Anybody can apply': Blanche pressed over who can get compensation from DOJ fund
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche was pressed during his testimony at a Senate Appropriations Subcommittee hearing over who could be compensated from the Justice Department’s anti-weaponization fund. Sen. Chris Coons, D-Del., questioned Blanche whether President Donald Trump’s campaign donors or those who assaulted law enforcement during the January 6 riots could receive compensation.

headlines.title: American Doctor Infected With Ebola While Working in Congo
headlines.description: The CDC confirms an American doctor has tested positive for the deadly Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This comes as entry into the United States is being restricted for some travelers in response to the outbreak. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump endorses Ken Paxton in Texas GOP Senate runoff
headlines.description: President Donald Trump announced he is endorsing Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn in the Republican primary runoff. NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin reports on the latest in the race.

headlines.title: Trump loyalty tests, major battlegrounds and more: What to watch in Tuesday’s elections
headlines.description: President Donald Trump’s campaign to suppress dissent in his own party faces its most prominent test yet as he takes aim at Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky, while GOP candidates across the country look to leverage the president’s endorsement in key primaries.

headlines.title: Teen Gunmen Open Fire in San Diego Mosque, Killing 3
headlines.description: Two teenaged gunmen opened fire at the Islamic Center of San Diego, killing three adults. Soon after, a vehicle was found with the bodies of both the alleged shooters inside. San Diego police tell NBC News that the mother of the 17-year-old suspect called police hours earlier, sharing she believed her son was suicidal, was wearing camouflage, left behind a note and that both her car and several firearms were missing. NBC’s Morgan Chesky reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Are consumers cracking under the weight of high prices? We’re about to find out
headlines.description: Home Depot, Lowe’s, Walmart and Target report earnings this week, offering a clear picture of how consumers are faring amid inflation and high energy costs.

headlines.title: Goodbye Trump, hello Putin: China’s Xi welcomes Russian leader facing pressure at home
headlines.description: Vladimir Putin landed in China on Tuesday to a warm welcome from his “dear friend” President Xi Jinping, but he arrived with the heavy burden of a war he increasingly seems unable to win.

headlines.title: Trump talks Iran while giving tour of White House ballroom construction
headlines.description: President Trump took questions from the press about the conflict with Iran after giving a tour of the White House ballroom construction site. NBC News' Gabe Gutierrez reports on the president's decision not to strike Iran and ballroom developments.

headlines.title: From The Sports Desk: Victor Wembanyama opens West finals with historic performance
headlines.description: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs beat the Thunder in a double-OT thriller to start the Western Conference finals

headlines.title: Wildfires, storms and record-breaking heat: Extreme weather advisories cover big swath of U.S.
headlines.description: Wildfires caused evacuation orders and warnings in Southern California, flood watches were in effect for 7 million people in the Midwest and 32 million in the Northeast were under heat advisories.

headlines.title: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger to sign order responding to federal agents at polls
headlines.description: Democratic Gov. Abigail Spanberger said Tuesday that she plans to issue an executive order on Wednesday to help election workers respond if federal agents show up at polling sites in Virginia.

headlines.title: NAACP calls for Black student-athletes to boycott Southern schools amid redistricting backlash
headlines.description: The NAACP is urging Black student-athletes to withhold their college commitments in states where new congressional maps could be drawn.
